File.role: GitHub profile page rendered at github.com/GatoGodMode

Section.purpose: GitHub NOTE alert: professional portfolio — not OSS program; proprietary career vs reviewable case studies and assurance excerpts

Open to Work + Portfolio badges; specialty tagline (Cyber Security · IAM · CRM); explicit target roles list; specialty shield row; IAM/CRM/security proof bullets; hiring-lane routing table; social badges; security discipline blockquote; LinkedIn CTA
Curated public evidence rationale: private proprietary career vs shareable IAM/CRM/security case studies
Primary hiring signal — collapsible: CRM/Zoho/banks-permitting; private builds; StrikeScope IAM; PDF/LuxuryCatalog/DNS-Sentinel tools; RoofRoofTexas case study with before/after assets
Tiered table: Tier 1 business systems/IAM/security first; Tier 2 shipped desktop assurance publications; collapsed research row
CRM/IAM-first tagline; compact badge row + Salesforce certs; full toolbox in collapsible details
Secondary collapsible deep dives: enterprise philosophy reframe; RawEngine/RawGraded/RawInvestor assurance focus; RawMarkets; collapsed TheMoun + RAW pipeline mermaid
No endorsement/partnership/employment disclaimer; portfolio not soliciting contributions
Target roles lead; LinkedIn primary CTA; shipped products secondary

Folder.role: RawGraded showcase screenshots for README expand section
RawInvestor Microsoft Store and Quick Card showcase screenshots for README expand section

File.role: Product whitepaper: thesis, EPIC rollout, engineering architecture, IP themes
Market research: fragmentation thesis, cited demand signals, grading economics
Mechanical ortho, system electrical schematic, OBJ reference mesh
Regenerates system electrical schematic SVG from engineering model

Folder.role: Structural and variable maps only; no other content permitted

Constraints.Rule: TheMoun engineering artifacts in docs/ are intentionally published for portfolio showcase; other repos may retain separate confidentiality rules.
Repo must remain named GatoGodMode to render as the profile README.
Local Windows paths in published docs use ROOT:\ for project roots and USER:\ for profile paths — never commit real drive letters or usernames.
